title: SRM Resting-state EEG
description: This dataset comprises resting-state electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 111 healthy control subjects acquired using a BioSemi ActiveTwo system with 64 electrodes. Subjects underwent four-minute eyes-closed resting-state EEG sessions, with a subset of participants having repeat recordings at a later timepoint. The raw data are provided with average reference and minimal preprocessing, alongside a cleaned derivative dataset processed through an automated pipeline and demographic/cognitive test data.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using a BioSemi ActiveTwo system with 64 electrodes positioned according to the extended 10-20 system (10-10). Each recording session consisted of four minutes of continuous resting-state EEG acquired with subjects' eyes closed. Raw signals were rereferenced to average reference without additional filtering. Cleaned derivative data were preprocessed using a fully automated pipeline including artifact detection and channel interpolation.
